t_ch: 铖杰，寒暄就不必了，你将我唤醒，一定是有重要的事情要问我，对吗？
t_en: Chengjie, let's cut to the chase. You have important questions for me, right?

t_ch: 莱恩老师，我从格物院里找到你最后的手稿，和入职邀请函放在一起，第一页背面写着「这是我一生的梦想」……
t_en: Master Ryan, I found your last manuscript in the Sapientia Academe stashed together with the IPC Designer Invite. At the back of the first page, it wrote, "This is the dream of my life"...

t_ch: 我记得，我将它放在抽屉的最深处。
t_en: I remember putting it deep in the drawer.

t_ch: 在老师您离开以后，我一直不敢面对这份手稿…直到最近，我才鼓起勇气将这份手稿当作是「最后的试卷」，想要造出图纸中的星槎。
t_en: I'd been avoiding the manuscript since you passed away... It was only until recently did I muster up the courage to treat the manuscript as the last test from you. I decided to have the starskiff drawn on the manuscript.

t_ch: 但是，无论是轻型的骨架、还是辅助驾驶系统…全都失败了。我明明是按照着师傅的手稿制作，却完全没有办法运行，我尝试了无数次，也失败了无数次。
t_en: Yet, I failed to build both the lightweight framework and the auxiliary driving system... I followed the instructions in your manuscript, but the things I built could not function at all. I have tried and failed for numerous times.

t_ch: 这份手稿是有问题的。
t_en: The manuscript is flawed.

t_ch: …怎么会这样？
t_en: ...How could it be?

t_ch: 手稿你带着吗？我来给你指出问题在哪儿。
t_en: Do you have the manuscript with you? I'll show you the errors.

t_ch: 铖杰将剩下的手稿递给了高大的金人，期待着金人指出问题所在。就像是一百多年前，师徒二人在工造司中的旧时光一样。
t_en: Chengjie hands the manuscript over to the enormous aurumaton, waiting to be enlightened. The two are reliving their good old times at the Artisanship Commission.

t_ch: 出乎意料地，高大的金人接过手稿，随即将它撕成了碎片；还未等铖杰师傅反应过来，纸片像飞起的花瓣一样，随着风离去了。
t_en: To everyone's surprise, the enormous aurumaton takes the manuscript and tears it into pieces. Before Chengjie can react, the pieces of paper have gone with the wind like flying flower petals.

t_ch: 老师！手稿——
t_en: Master! The manuscript...

t_ch: 不许捡起来。
t_en: Don't pick them up.

t_ch: 可这是您最后的手稿，也是我出师的最后机会——
t_en: It's your last manuscript and my last chance at mastering craftsmanship...

t_ch: 那只是一个很蠢的设计，根本就没有完成。即便你是长生种，也不应该将时间浪费在这叠破纸上，那不过是我学艺尚浅时不成熟的作品。
t_en: That's nothing but a dumb and incomplete design. Even though you're a long-life species, you shouldn't waste your time on it. That's a poor design I made when I was still a novice.

t_ch: 可是，这份手稿不是您一生的梦想吗？
t_en: But isn't it the dream of your life?

t_ch: 没错，它曾经是我的梦想，你愿意为我实现梦想，我很感动。
t_en: Yes, it used to be my dream. I'm very touched that you're willing to fulfill it for me.

t_ch: 但你只看到了「梦想」，却一点也没有看出手稿粗陋与青涩……铖杰，很遗憾，你没有成为星槎匠人的才能。
t_en: You focus too much on that concept that you overlook the weaknesses and inferiority of the manuscript... Chengjie, I'm very sorry to say that you aren't cut out for being a starskiff craftsman.

t_ch: （这是不是有些太严重了。）
t_en: (Isn't that a bit too harsh?)

t_ch: （我其实也这样觉得。）
t_en: (I think so too.)

t_ch: 我第一次见到你的时候，你满腔热情，就像是当初来到仙舟求学的自己一样。
t_en: When I first met you, you were full of passion. You reminded me of myself when I first came to Xianzhou to learn.

t_ch: 其他师傅只看重才能，而我看重学习的热情，所以我愿意收你为徒。还记得么，我们俩毫无保留的传授与学习、互相鼓励，那是我在工造司里最快乐的时光。
t_en: Unlike other masters who emphasized talent, I valued passion for learning. That was why I took you in as my disciple. Do you remember how we used to exchange ideas and encourage each other? That was my happiest moment at the Artisanship Commission.

t_ch: 但你自己看看，你那最宝贵的热情，被我那粗陋的梦想牢牢捆住，快要磨干耗尽了…我从来没想过事情会发展成这样。
t_en: Now, look at yourself. Your enthusiasm is fettered by my poor design and is about to be depleted... I never thought that things would pan out this way.

t_ch: 我什么也帮不了你啦，既不能指点你技术，也不能说些什么好听的话继续骗你浪费时间。
t_en: There's nothing I can do to help you. I can neither guide you nor say pleasant things to trick you into wasting more of your time.

t_ch: 所以，为师能为你做到的最后一件事就是——撕毁手稿。
t_en: So, destroying the manuscript is the last thing I can do for you.

t_ch: 可是，今后…今后我该怎么办呢？
t_en: But, wh—what should I do in the future?

t_ch: 你已经比我去世时年长太多了，今后的路，你应该问问你自己。
t_en: You're so much older than I was when I passed away. Hence, you should follow your heart.

t_ch: 换个行当吧，和我不一样，身为仙舟人，你还有很多很多的时间……
t_en: Why don't you try another career? Unlike me, you're a Xianzhou citizen with plenty of time ahead of you...

t_ch: 巨大的金人不再动作，回到了最初的沉默中。
t_en: The enormous aurumaton stops moving and falls silent like how it was at the beginning.

t_ch: *机械停止运转的声音*
t_en: *Whirring noise of a machine that stops working*

t_ch: 他不在了。它已重返因果殿中了。
t_en: He isn't here. He's returned to the Hall of Karma.

t_ch: 是要我把金人带走是吗，不是？算了，我有点混乱。
t_en: Are you asking me to take the aurumaton away? Sorry, my mind is a mess now.

t_ch: …我只是需要一个人冷静一下，抱歉。
t_en: ...I just need some time to get hold of myself.

t_ch: 你来啦，刚才我还在和青雀小姐商量，要是不做工匠，我能去做什么。
t_en: Hi there. I was discussing with Miss Qingque what I could do besides being a craftsman.

t_ch: 我们从飞行士聊到职业牌手，还谈到了厨师和编剧，铖杰师傅说自己都没有什么自信。
t_en: We touched on occupations like the pilot, professional celestial jade player, chef, and even scriptwriter. Yet, Chengjie was not sure if any of those is suitable for him.

t_ch: 真的要改行吗？
t_en: Are you sure you want to change your career?

t_ch: 我一直以为自己天生就是工匠的命了，虽然命苦一点儿，但从来没想过做别的行当。
t_en: I've always considered myself born to be a craftsman. Although the job is tough, the idea of finding another job never crosses my mind.

t_ch: 现在，我想试试别的工作。
t_en: Now, I'd like to give other careers a shot.

t_ch: 我看太卜司司库就挺闲的。
t_en: The Divination Commission custodian seems to be an easy gig.

t_ch: 司库其实还是很有技术含量的，请两位不要来抢我饭碗。
t_en: Well, it actually requires a lot of skills to be a custodian. Please stop eyeing my job, you two.

t_ch: 当初是为什么要去做工匠呢？不会也像我一样，是家里逼着我去找个铁饭碗的吧。
t_en: What motivated you to become a craftsman back then? Did your parents urge you to find a stable job like mine?

t_ch: 一开始的时候，是觉得设计师画出干净的设计稿很帅气，自己也想画出这样的设计稿…结果这些年，画倒是越画越好看，但是设计却完全没设计。
t_en: At first, I thought it was cool to be able to come up with a well-drawn design, so I was inspired to do the same... In the end, I only became better at drawing without creating my own design.

t_ch: 那这样的话，铖杰师傅要不试试「绘画」吧？
t_en: If that's the case, should you give painting a try?

t_ch: 绘画吗？我这些年只画过各种各样的设计稿，我这水平真的可以吗？
t_en: Painting? I've only drawn design manuscripts before. Will I be good enough to be a painter?

t_ch: 没有人一开始是大师。
t_en: No one is born a master.

t_ch: 我明白你的意思，先从学徒做起，没错吧？
t_en: I understand what you mean. I should start from being an apprentice, right?

t_ch: 别画画了，还是找个厂打工吧。
t_en: Painting isn't a good idea. You should find a factory job instead.

t_ch: 我明白，人活着更重要。唉，但…人生哪能没有个盼头呢？
t_en: You've got a point. It's more important to do something that can earn me a living. But how can life be without passion?

t_ch: 机器都能画画了，你找不到工作的。
t_en: Machines are capable of drawing now. You won't find a job as a painter.

t_ch: …哎，看来各行各业都不容易，但这样望而却步的话，什么都没法开始。
t_en: ...Alas, it seems like there are no easy jobs in the world. If I keep hesitating, I won't be able to achieve anything.

t_ch: 好吧，我听从你们的建议，就从我感兴趣的「绘画」开始。
t_en: Well, I'll heed your advice and start exploring my passion — painting.

t_ch: …自从没有了「师傅的梦想」之后，我好像也没有失去什么。
t_en: ...I don't think I've lost anything after dropping my master's dream.

t_ch: 反倒是因为没有了自责和愧疚，没有了突然的奋斗和努力，好像从背上卸下了一座大山，感觉自己轻飘飘的。
t_en: Without the guilt and the obligation to do my best, it's like a weight off my shoulders. My body feels light and airy.

t_ch: 你这个人责任感也太重了。
t_en: You have such a strong sense of responsibility.

t_ch: 我记得自己在迴星港的时候说过——
t_en: I remember saying this when I was in Stargazer Navalia...

t_ch: 「总有一天，我也要将我师傅的梦想之船造出来，轻轻将它推出去，让它在星槎海最繁荣的航道里飞行！」
t_en: "One day, I'll build my master's dream starskiff, push it out gently, and set it sailing in the busiest lane of Starskiff Haven!"

t_ch: 铖杰将折好的小纸船，送进星槎海风里，轻轻地将它推出去，看着它在星槎海最繁荣的航道里飞行。
t_en: Chengjie holds a paper boat amid the blowing wind by the Starskiff Haven. Then, he gives the paper boat a push and sets it sailing in the busiest lane of the sea.

t_ch: 你们共同注视着纸船越飞越远，直到消失在视线之中……
t_en: Together you stare at the paper boat as it flies until it vanishes from sight...

t_ch: <size=36>师傅，谢谢你，今天我出师了！</size>
t_en: <size=36>Master, thank you. I've graduated today!</size>

t_ch: 若能摄取完好的神识…
t_en: That is, if his soul essence is still intact for extraction...

t_ch: 十王司便有把握追根溯源
t_en: The Ten-Lords Commission will be able to trace his roots.

t_ch: 可惜…
t_en: What a pity...

t_ch: 一艘星槎模样的纸船静静地躺在栏杆上，就像是经历了兜兜转转、世事浮沉之后，再次回到了最初的地方。
t_en: A paper boat in the shape of a starskiff rests peacefully on the railing. It looks like it has returned to its origin after going through ups and downs and twists and turns.

t_ch: 再次出航，又何尝不可呢？
t_en: Nothing can stop it from going on another voyage, right?

t_ch: 一番周折，你们终于完成了任务。
t_en: After some twists and turns, you have finally completed the mission.

t_ch: 成了，穷观阵会以这些人为线索，追查勾连，这些叛徒谁也逃不了。
t_en: That's it. The Matrix of Prescience will use these people as clues to hunt down the others. None of these traitors can escape.

t_ch: 所以接下来，该是判官大人兑现承诺的时候了？
t_en: Now, is it time for you to realize your promise, Judge Xueyi?

t_ch: 还阳之事，可没那么简单。
t_en: Bringing someone back to the world of living is a complicated matter.

t_ch: 你还有什么要求？
t_en: What else do you want from us?

t_ch: 怎么，说话不算话？
t_en: Are you going back on your word?

t_ch: 汝师作古已逾百年，要将他的神识自因果殿深处带回，要劳烦太卜以穷观阵索引定位……
t_en: Your master has been dead for more than a century. Therefore, the master diviner will need to locate your master using the Matrix of Prescience to bring him out from the depth of the Hall of Karma.

t_ch: 这件事就包在我身上好了。
t_en: Leave it to me.

t_ch: 此外，已逝之人的身躯不复存在。若欲令其开口，还需一具机巧之身充作形骸，就像吾身一样，希望汝等能够速速备妥。
t_en: Besides, the deceased don't have a physical body. To allow your master to speak, he'll need to possess an ingenium body like me. Please get it ready as soon as possible.

t_ch: 铖杰师傅快去准备金人。
t_en: Chengjie, please prepare an aurumaton.

t_ch: 啊！我明白了，刚好就有一台，我现在就把它带过来！
t_en: Yes, I happen to have one now. Let me bring it over!

t_ch: 不能凭附在雪衣大人身上吗？
t_en: Can't his master's soul be attached to your body, Madam Xueyi?

t_ch: 吾身归属十王司，公器私用，容吾拒绝。
t_en: Given that my body belongs to the Ten-Lords Commission, I will never use it for personal purposes.

t_ch: 是说金人吗？我明白了，刚好就有一台，我现在就把它带过来！
t_en: Do you mean an aurumaton? Yes, I happen to have one now. Let me bring it over!

t_ch: 本座这可是念在{NICKNAME}是仙舟贵客，才出借穷观阵的。相对的，今后若是有需要{NICKNAME}的时候……
t_en: I'm only willing to lend the Matrix of Prescience because {NICKNAME} is our honored guest. And, if I need {NICKNAME}'s help in the future...

t_ch: 我也会提供帮助的。
t_en: I'll help you out.

t_ch: 不错，要是将军没有看错人的话，本座还得多卖点人情给你才是。
t_en: Great. If the general is right with his judgment, I shall do more favors for you.

t_ch: 我这也太亏了吧。
t_en: This is a bad bargain for me.

t_ch: 互帮互助之事，哪里还有盈亏的计较，依本座看，我们是都赚了。
t_en: You shouldn't think about personal gains or losses when you're helping others. In my view, this is a win-win situation for us.

t_ch: 与那时一样，本座会在这「穷观阵」的中心引导阵法。你们要做的，是将那座三座阵基重启。去吧，可别让判官久等了。
t_en: Like the previous time, I'll stay in the middle of this Matrix of Prescience to control it. What you need to do now is reactivate the three base terminals. Go on now. Don't make the judge wait too long.

t_ch: 就按雪衣判官所说的照办吧，本座就在阵心等待你们回来。
t_en: Follow Judge Xueyi's instructions. And I shall be waiting for you here at the Matrix core.

t_ch: 目前所追逃的犯人，就在这份名单里……
t_en: The fugitives are on this list...

t_ch: 迴星港内罪囚流窜，将彼等活着带回来，再来谈谈还阳之事。
t_en: You can find many of them roaming around at Stargazer Navalia. Bring them back alive, then we'll talk about your master.

t_ch: 你先去零件区翻翻，那里应该有一些可用的金人零件，我去借一台金人过来，再看看要怎么做才能把技术移植上去……
t_en: You go check the parts area. There should be some usable aurumaton parts there. I'll go borrow an aurumaton and see how I can transfer the technology over...

t_ch: 你们要做的，是将那座三座阵基重启。去吧，可别让判官久等了。
t_en: What you need to do now is to reactivate the three base terminals. Go on now. Don't make the judge wait too long.

t_ch: 因果殿中的魂魄无穷无尽，需要借助大衍穷观阵锚定逝者，请尽快调整阵法。
t_en: As there are countless souls in the Hall of Karma, the Matrix of Prescience Ultima is required to locate the deceased. Please adjust the matrix immediately.

t_ch: 法眼预见了这个结局。虽然有些伤感，甚至有点残酷，但这是最好的做法。
t_en: The omniscia has predicted this. Although it's saddening, or even somewhat cruel, it's the best solution.

t_ch: 这确实是更优做法。
t_en: It's indeed the better solution.

t_ch: 如果不是最敬重的老师亲自开口，他什么也听不进去。他还会用「梦想」为借口继续欺骗自己。
t_en: He wouldn't have listened to anyone except his master, whom he respected the most. He would continue using the excuse of his master's dream and refuse to give up.

t_ch: 唉，早知如此，不召唤他来…
t_en: If I'd known that, I wouldn't have summoned his master...

t_ch: 呵呵，本座倒是觉得，莱恩老师授业解惑，尽了自己最后的导师职责，是个不错的老师啊。
t_en: Hehe, I see Master Ryan as a good teacher. After all, he has already done his duty as a teacher by answering his student's questions.

t_ch: 时候不早了，{NICKNAME}，去陪陪那位学徒吧。
t_en: It's getting late, {NICKNAME}. Why don't you spend some time with the apprentice?

t_ch: 逝者已经回到了因果殿，还阳一事还请守秘。
t_en: The deceased has returned to the Hall of Karma. Please don't tell anyone about what happened earlier.

t_ch: 我觉得莱恩老师说的没错，因为我不太相信「实现某个人的梦想」这种事情。
t_en: I agree with what Master Ryan said, as I think there's no such thing as helping someone fulfill their dream.

t_ch: 你的梦想是你的梦想，我的梦想是我的梦想，你帮我实现了梦想，我也没能实现梦想……
t_en: My dream only belongs to me. Even if someone helps me realize it, it isn't the same as doing it myself.

t_ch: …这是不是自我感动呀？
t_en: ...Do people do that to make themselves feel better?

t_ch: 这不是自我感动。
t_en: No.

t_ch: 好吧。
t_en: Alright.

t_ch: 这确实是自我感动。
t_en: Yes.

t_ch: 现在回想起来，铖杰师傅一直将「师傅的梦想」挂在嘴边，那他自己的梦想又是什么呢？
t_en: Come to think of it, Chengjie only talks about his master's dream. How about his dream?

t_ch: 哎，应该早点问问他的。
t_en: Alas, we should have asked him that earlier.

t_ch: 金人不再动作，也不再言语。
t_en: The aurumaton stops moving and falls silent.

t_ch: 铖杰师傅好像给你准备了小礼物，去问问看吧。
t_en: Chengjie seems to have prepared a gift for you. You can ask him about it.

t_ch: 我还有事儿，在这里待一会儿就回去了。
t_en: I'll take my leave soon to attend to something.

t_ch: 这个纸船还剩一个，送给你好了。
t_en: There's one more paper boat left. You can have it.

t_ch: （将纸船送进航道里）
t_en: (Push the paper boat and send it onto a lane)

t_ch: 再见了，星槎。
t_en: Goodbye, starskiff.

t_ch: 再见了，星槎。
t_en: Goodbye, starskiff.

t_ch: （沉默）
t_en: (Remain Silent)

t_ch: （将纸船留作纪念）
t_en: (Keep the paper boat as a memento)

t_ch: 这样也好，谢谢你。
t_en: That's great too. Thank you.

t_ch: 我们以后有缘再见吧。
t_en: Let's hope we'll cross paths again in the future.

t_ch: 你不去看看吗，希儿？这可是那小子的卫冕战哪。
t_en: Aren't you going to go watch the fight, Seele? He's defending his championship title.

t_ch: …还是算了吧。反正有他在，冠军也落不到别人头上。
t_en: ...Nah, I'll pass. It's not like anyone else has a chance with him around.

t_ch: 哈哈哈，说的也是！你不想去凑热闹我也能理解，毕竟场面每次也都大差不差。
t_en: Hahaha, you're right about that! I get it if you don't wanna join in the fun. It's all the same after a while.

t_ch: 哎，难得的清闲…我是不是也该找个地方活动活动筋骨了？
t_en: Ahh, what a rare moment of rest... Maybe I should find a spot to stretch my legs a bit, too.

t_ch: （希儿和奥列格…他们俩在聊什么呢？）
t_en: (Seele and Oleg... I wonder what they're talking about?)

t_ch: （上去问问吧。）
t_en: (I'll go ask them.)

t_ch: 唷，这不是{NICKNAME}吗！有阵子没见你了，最近还好吧？
t_en: Hey, isn't that the trailblazer? It's been a while since I've seen you around. How you doing?

t_ch: 嗨！旅行还顺利吗？多亏了你们，我跟头儿最近都快闲出毛病了。
t_en: Hey! How have your travels been? Thanks to you, things are pretty peaceful around here. The chief and I are getting restless!

t_ch: 哈哈哈，这丫头！好不容易得享两天清福，被你说得跟什么坏事一样！
t_en: Hahaha, silly girl! Just a few days of hard-earned rest, and you're already making it sound like jail time!

t_ch: 你们刚才在聊什么呢？
t_en: What were you two chatting about?

t_ch: 噢，你听见了？
t_en: Oh, you heard us?

t_ch: 我们刚才在聊我徒弟，卢卡。
t_en: We were talking about my student, Luka.

t_ch: 有点怀念磐岩镇的温馨了。
t_en: I'm kinda missing Boulder Town's homey feeling.

t_ch: …温馨？真不知道你后来都经历了些什么。
t_en: ...Homey? Sheesh, you must've gone through a lot since leaving here...

t_ch: 看来咱这淳朴热情的民风给客人留下了不错的印象啊，哈哈哈。
t_en: Sounds like our rustic and friendly community left a good impression on the visitors, hahaha.

t_ch: 你们刚才在聊什么呢？
t_en: What were you two chatting about?

t_ch: 噢，你听见了？
t_en: Oh, you heard us?

t_ch: 我们刚才在聊我徒弟，卢卡。
t_en: We were talking about my student, Luka.

t_ch: 你之前应该跟他打过照面了吧？对，就是那个装了只机械胳膊的小子。他之前也一直在为「地火」办事，只是不在这个镇上。
t_en: You've probably met him before, right? Yep, it's that boy with the mechanical arm. He was also doing work for Wildfire before, just not in this town.

t_ch: 哎，对了——{NICKNAME}肯定还没见过卢卡那小子守擂吧？希儿，不如你带着咱们的大英雄去搏击俱乐部尝尝鲜，怎么样？
t_en: Oh, right — {F#she}{M#he} hasn't seen Luka defend his championship title before, right? Seele, why don't you take our hero and swing by the Fight Club to show {F#her}{M#him} the match?
Uh...

t_ch: 怎么了，不愿意？
t_en: What? You don't want to?

t_ch: …不，这没啥。我只是怕卢卡那家伙又想一出是一出。
t_en: ...No, it's just... I'm worried Luka might do something stupid again.

t_ch: 以前发生过什么吗？
t_en: ...Again?

t_ch: 带我去嘛，带我去嘛！
t_en: Come on! Take me! I wanna go! Pleaaase~

t_ch: 你正常点……
t_en: Ugh... How old are you again?

t_ch: 噢，我好像想起来了…之前有一次卢卡守擂，希儿就在下面当观众。
t_en: Oh, I remember now... Luka was defending his title once, and Seele was watching in the audience.

t_ch: 那一场的对手跟纸糊的似的，才挨了两拳就趴下了。卢卡那小子不知道吃错了什么药，当场指名要希儿上台跟他过两招……
t_en: The opponent for that fight was too weak. They went down with just a few punches. I don't know what got into Luka after that, but he said out loud that he wanted Seele to come up and fight him right there and then...

t_ch: …要不就说到这儿吧，头儿。我可不想回忆那么尴尬的场景。
t_en: ...Why don't we stop there, chief. I'd rather not relive the moment.

t_ch: 哈哈哈，行！那我就此打住。反正那小子就是这性子，气氛一到就容易忘乎所以。
t_en: Hahaha, okay, I'll stop. Anyway, that's what Luka's like. He forgets about himself once he's in the mood.

t_ch: …不过我相信卢卡，同样的错误他不会犯两次的。所以回到刚才的提议，丫头——你就带{NICKNAME}去搏击俱乐部看看吧？
t_en: ...But I trust Luka not to make the same mistake again. So back to the original suggestion — are you good to take {NICKNAME} to the Fight Club, Seele?

t_ch: …行吧，那我带你过去。
t_en: ...Fine. I'll take you there.

t_ch: 这就对了，这广交朋友多是一件美事！希望你跟我的爱徒能合得来，{NICKNAME}。
t_en: That's more like it. The more friends the merrier! I hope you can get along with my favorite student.

t_ch: 哦，你应该还没见过他吧？简而言之，那小子是下层区一流的格斗家，之前也一直在为「地火」办事，只是不在这个镇上。
t_en: Oh, you probably haven't met him yet. In short, he's a top-tier fighter in the Underworld. He was also doing work for Wildfire before, just not in this town.

t_ch: 我…不行了，我认输……
t_en: That's it... I give up...
...Hmph.

t_ch: 下层…啊不，贝洛伯格的朋友们！第一千七百六十一届搏击擂主挑战赛冠军，参赛三十次、优胜二十八次的磐岩拳王——卢卢卢卢卢卡！
t_en: Friends of the Under— I mean — friends of Belobog! The champion of the <unbreak>1761</unbreak>st Fighting King Challenge, <unbreak>30</unbreak> tournaments, <unbreak>28</unbreak> victories, the Boulder King of Fists... Luuuuuuuukaaaaaaaa!

t_ch: …看来已经结束了。
t_en: ...Looks like it's over already.
...Hehe.

t_ch: 又守擂成功了——恭喜你啊，卢卡。
t_en: Another title defended — congrats, Luka.

t_ch: 谢了，希儿！这位是…噢，是{NICKNAME}对吧？我记着你呢。
t_en: Thanks, Seele! And who might... Oh, it's {NICKNAME}! I remember you.

t_ch: 你们是专程来看我比赛的吗？那这排场可真不小。
t_en: Were you here just to see my fight? Well, I'm flattered.

t_ch: 我不过是赢了一场小小的拳赛而已——你们俩的事迹可比这伟大多了。我们……
t_en: I just won a little fight in the cage — what you did was much bigger than that. We...

t_ch: 卢卡——卢卡——卢卡——
t_en: Luka — Luka — Luka —

t_ch: …他们还真不知道累啊。
t_en: ...The crowd never gets tired, do they?

t_ch: 我们要不去外面吧？这里的氛围不太适合说话。
t_en: Do you wanna head outside? It's a bit loud for talking in here.

t_ch: 下次再见，伙计们——
t_en: See you next time, everyone...

t_ch: ——「等着见识火花四溅吧！」
t_en: ...there are sparks yet to fly!

t_ch: 唷！希儿，还有…呃，这位是……
t_en: Hey! Seele! And, um, you are...?

t_ch: {NICKNAME}，幸会。
t_en: {NICKNAME}. Pleasure to meet you.

t_ch: （看向希儿）
t_en: (Look at Seele)

t_ch: …哦，忘了介绍，这位就是{NICKNAME}，{F#她}{M#他}的名字你肯定听过。
t_en: ...Oh, I forgot to introduce {F#her}{M#him}. This is the trailblazer. You must have heard of {F#her}{M#him} before.

t_ch: …原来这位就是帮助上下层重建了联系的「开拓者」？真是幸会！
t_en: ...So this is the trailblazer who helped reunite the Overworld with the Underworld? It's truly a pleasure to meet you!

t_ch: 总算没人跟着咱们了——就在这儿吧！
t_en: Finally, I think we lost them... Let's chat here!

t_ch: 还是一如既往地受欢迎啊，「冠军」。
t_en: You're popular as ever, "champion."

t_ch: …你还在为那事生我的气啊，希儿？我那就是一时上头……
t_en: ...Are you still angry at me over that one time, Seele? I just got a bit carried away...

t_ch: 所以你们打了一场？
t_en: So, you two had a fight?

t_ch: …当然没有，两个「地火」的干部在搏击俱乐部里大打出手，那场面不管怎么想都有点离谱吧？
t_en: Of course not. Two senior members of Wildfire duking it out in the Fight Club? That would be bad form.

t_ch: 你们在说啥…
t_en: What are you two talking about...?

t_ch: …没什么，就是奥列格头儿之前说的那事。
t_en: ...Nothing. Just that thing Chief Oleg mentioned before.

t_ch: 反正后来我们也没真打起来。两个「地火」的干部在俱乐部里大打出手…那场面怎么想都有点离谱吧？
t_en: It's not like we actually fought. Two senior members of Wildfire duking it out in the Fight Club? That would be bad form.

t_ch: 当时现场的气氛那么热烈，结果决赛没几回合就分出胜负了——我看大伙儿都意犹未尽，然后就脑子一热…你就别记仇了，希儿。
t_en: The mood was on fire that day, but the final match was over in just a few swings — everyone wanted more. So, in the heat of the moment, I just... Come on — don't hold that against me, Seele.

t_ch: 再说了，我是真的佩服你的实力，所以才一直想跟你过两招。
t_en: I've always been impressed by you — I've wanted to spar with you for ages.

t_ch: …我不喜欢抱着玩闹的心态和人动手，你还是别指望了。
t_en: ...I don't fight for fun. Stop getting your hopes up.

t_ch: 话说回来，你特意把我们揪到外面，是有什么话想说吗？
t_en: Anyway, now that you've dragged us out here, what did you wanna talk about?

t_ch: 噢，正是！实话告诉你们，我正着手筹备一个计划……
t_en: Ah, yes! So, I'm trying to get a plan going...

t_ch: 计划？
t_en: Plan?

t_ch: 对——准确的说，是我个人的修炼计划。
t_en: Yes — more accurately, my personal training plan.

t_ch: 和我有关吗？
t_en: Anything to do with me?

t_ch: 嗯？那倒没什么关系，这是我个人的修炼计划。
t_en: Hm? Not really. It's my personal training plan.

t_ch: 我想请你们两位加入我，共同见证卢卡——磐岩镇的搏击冠军——下一步的成长！
t_en: I want you to join me in witnessing the next steps in the development of Luka, the Boulder Town fighting champion!

t_ch: 喂喂，你给我等一会儿！奥列格头儿只是让我们来替你加油…你倒好，一拍脑袋就想拉我们当陪练？
t_en: Whoa, whoa, hold your horses. Chief Oleg wanted us to come and cheer for you, he didn't say anything about becoming your sparring partners.

t_ch: 我们可没那个时间……
t_en: We haven't got the time...

t_ch: 哈哈哈，你的表情真是完全藏不住。师傅都跟我说了，下层区的局势最近相对稳定，「地火」的日子都变清闲了。
t_en: Hahaha, you can't hide anything on that face of yours. Oleg already told me that things in the Underworld are pretty peaceful now, and even Wildfire is running out of things to do.

t_ch: 而且，你看{NICKNAME}的眼神，{F#她}{M#他}肯定很感兴趣，对吧？
t_en: Besides, look at {NICKNAME}! You want in, right?

t_ch: 确实有点…
t_en: You got me...

t_ch: 我果然没看错！
t_en: I knew it!

t_ch: 我也有的是时间。
t_en: I do have plenty of time...

t_ch: 我果然没看错！
t_en: I knew it!

t_ch: 主角的宿命就是当工具人…
t_en: Protagonist, at your service...

t_ch: …这又是什么奇怪的台词？
t_en: ...That might be the weirdest thing you've ever said.

t_ch: 就是你不会拒绝的意思，对吧？我果然没看错！
t_en: So that's a yes, right? I knew it!

t_ch: 哎…总是自说自话，拿你没办法。行吧，既然{NICKNAME}答应了，那这趟也算我一个。
t_en: *sigh* There's no stopping you once you've made up your mind. Fine. Since {NICKNAME} agreed, count me in too.

t_ch: 好，那就这么定了！这次修炼的地点就选在史瓦罗的地盘——你们之前干的大事我都有耳闻，你俩对那边的环境应该熟悉得很吧？
t_en: Great! That's settled then! The training is on Svarog's turf. I heard about your achievements — you probably know that place like the back of your hand, right?

t_ch: 等你准备好了就告诉我一声，咱们一起出发！
t_en: Just lemme know when you're ready and we can head over together!

t_ch: 怎么样，{NICKNAME}？准备好陪我走一遭特训之旅了吗？
t_en: How about it, {NICKNAME}? Are you ready to head out for the special training?

t_ch: 出发吧。
t_en: Let's go.

t_ch: 这就准备好了？那我们出发！
t_en: That was fast! Let's do it!

t_ch: 跟好了，希儿，可别掉队！
t_en: Keep up, Seele! Don't fall behind!

t_ch: …才不用你提醒，走吧。
t_en: ...I don't need you to remind me. Let's go.

t_ch: 让我再准备准备。
t_en: I might need some more prep time...

t_ch: 嗐，别紧张！我保证，这一趟对你来说绝对轻松又愉快。
t_en: Ha, don't stress! I promise this trip will be relaxing and fun.

t_ch: 不着急，那等你准备好了再来找我吧。
t_en: Take your time. Come back when you're ready.

t_ch: 卢卡…能听见吗？
t_en: Luka... can you hear me?

t_ch: 喂…喂！起床了，卢卡！
t_en: Hey... Hey! Luka, you're up!

t_ch: 呃…唔……
t_en: Ugh... I...

t_ch: 呵，总算醒了！你这一躺就是一整天，可把我们给吓坏了。
t_en: Ha, you're finally awake! You've been out for the whole day. We were really worried!

t_ch: 别担心，希儿，他只是体力不支晕过去了。看得出来，史瓦罗这次刻意没下狠手。
t_en: Don't worry, Seele. Like I said, it was just the exhaustion. Svarog was deliberately holding back.

t_ch: 希儿…{NICKNAME}…还有…娜塔大姐头？我这是…在诊所里？
t_en: Seele... {NICKNAME}... and... Nat? I'm in... the clinic?

t_ch: …不然呢？难道我们就把你晾在史瓦罗的老家不管了？
t_en: ...Where else? You think we'd leave you on Svarog's turf?

t_ch: 哈…谢谢你们，我感觉好多了。真不好意思，娜塔…浪费你的精力了。
t_en: Ha... thank you all. I feel much better now. Sorry, Nat... I must have wasted your time.

t_ch: 别说傻话，卢卡。不管你是出于什么用心，下次都别再干这种傻事了。
t_en: Don't be ridiculous, Luka. Still, whatever your motivation was, I hope you can find a different outlet for it.

t_ch: …我可没娜塔这么好说话，这事不能就这么过去了。趁这功夫，你赶紧解释一下，之前到底是哪根筋搭错了？
t_en: ...I'm not gonna let you off the hook that easy — there's more to this story. You need to explain what the heck got into you today.

t_ch: 史瓦罗下手也够狠的。
t_en: Svarog hit pretty hard.

t_ch: 都说了史瓦罗是机器人…就算他再怎么会计算，也很难把下手轻重控制得正正好好吧？
t_en: Like I said, he's a robot... No matter how good his calculations are, he's not designed to pull punches.

t_ch: 你之前的「胜利口号」…
t_en: That victory slogan of yours...

t_ch: 你晕过去的唯一好处，就是我们不用再听你喊一遍了……
t_en: The one good thing about you losing is that we didn't have to hear it again.

t_ch: 为什么非要和史瓦罗打不可？
t_en: Why did you insist on fighting Svarog?

t_ch: 这也怪我…我就该直接拒绝你的。我从一开始就有种不好的预感。
t_en: That's on me too... I should've refused him straightaway. I had a bad feeling from the start.

t_ch: 哈哈…这么较真，是你的风格。我都说了，这就是我的试炼计…咳咳、咳！
t_en: Hahaha... you're always so serious. I've said it before — this is my train— *coughing*

t_ch: …别乱动，你还没完全恢复好。希儿，你也别逼得太紧了…卢卡现在需要休息。
t_en: ...Slow down — you haven't completely recovered. Seele, don't pressure him... let him rest.

t_ch: 谢谢你，娜塔——不过我已经恢复得差不多了。
t_en: Thanks, Nat — but I'm almost recovered.

t_ch: 我现在…还有点事得去办。嘿……
t_en: I have... some things to do... Ah...

t_ch: 喂，你这家伙——
t_en: Hey, where do you think you're—

t_ch: …让他去吧，希儿。
t_en: ...Let him go, Seele.

t_ch: 真搞不懂那家伙……
t_en: I really don't get him...

t_ch: 哥们儿也许只是中二病犯了。
t_en: Bro is just being a bit of a weeb.

t_ch: …什么病？
t_en: What's that supposed to mean?

t_ch: 我也想不明白。
t_en: I don't get the whole thing, either.

t_ch: …能想明白才怪吧？
t_en: ...I don't think anybody gets it.

t_ch: 他可能有什么不能说的原因。
t_en: Maybe he has his reasons.

t_ch: …这可算不上理由。
t_en: ...Well whatever they are, they're not good enough.

t_ch: 要是他打一开始就没打算把事挑明白，那为啥还非要拉上咱俩？不行，这也太奇怪了——我得跟着他，看看那家伙到底在打什么主意。
t_en: If he never intended to explain anything to us, why did he drag us along? No, this is too weird — I'm gonna follow him and see what he's up to.

t_ch: …有这个必要吗？
t_en: ...Is that really necessary?

t_ch: …我实在放不下这个心。卢卡那家伙，以前从来不会这样。
t_en: ...I can't just let it go. Luka didn't use to be like this.

t_ch: {NICKNAME}，你最近好像不忙？要是需要帮手，我随时联系你。
t_en: ...You're not too busy these days, right? I'll give you a shout if I need help.

t_ch: 记得告诉我进展。
t_en: Keep me updated.

t_ch: 当然。你最近好像不忙？要是需要帮手，我随时联系你。
t_en: Of course. You're not too busy these days, right? I'll give you a shout if I need help.

t_ch: 卢卡那家伙，以前从来不会这样…这个心我实在放不下。
t_en: Luka didn't use to be like this... I can't just let it go.

t_ch: 你去吧，希儿。别太着急了…我可不想看到你们吵架。
t_en: If you must, Seele. Try not to worry... I don't want to see you two fighting.

t_ch: 放心吧，娜塔。
t_en: Don't worry, Nat.

t_ch: …{NICKNAME}，既然他们两个都走了，我有一事相求。
t_en: ...{NICKNAME}, now that they've left, I have a favor to ask you.

t_ch: 据我了解，卢卡那孩子虽然有时会热情过了头…但他绝对不是个莽撞的人。他这次的行为…确实有些反常。
t_en: It's easy for Luka to get ahead of himself sometimes... but I've never seen him this headstrong. It does make me wonder...

t_ch: 你能替我去找奥列格问问卢卡的近况吗？我毕竟没有亲自经历，不了解事情的全貌。
t_en: Could you ask Oleg how Luka's been recently? I haven't seen much of him lately — it's hard for me to get the full picture.

t_ch: 既然你都问了…
t_en: Since you asked...

t_ch: 我正有这个打算。
t_en: I was planning on doing just that.

t_ch: 那太好了。不管发生了什么，我都希望你们能好好相处，不要被一些琐事影响了彼此间的关系。回见，{NICKNAME}。
t_en: Thank you. Regardless of what's really going on, I hope none of you let small things get in the way of your friendship. See you, {NICKNAME}.

t_ch: 麻烦你去向奥列格打听一下情况了，{NICKNAME}。
t_en: Please go ask Oleg about this, {NICKNAME}

t_ch: 希望你们能好好相处，不要被一些琐事影响了彼此间的关系。
t_en: Don't let small things get in the way of your friendship.

t_ch: 关于卢卡，有些事想问你。
t_en: I wanna ask you some things about Luka.

t_ch: …哦？我徒弟？你们后来又发生什么了吗？
t_en: ...Oh? My student? Did something happen recently?

t_ch: 我正纳闷呢，怎么冠军赛结束这么久他还没来找我——来，快给我讲讲。
t_en: I was wondering why he didn't come and see me after the championship match ended. Come, tell me about it.

t_ch: 把和希儿、卢卡一同经历的事情转述给了奥列格……
t_en: You tell Oleg about everything that happened with Luka and Seele...

t_ch: 嗯…听你这么一说，确实有不少奇怪的地方，完全不像他的作风……
t_en: Mm... that does sound a little strange. Luka usually doesn't push himself so far...

t_ch: …我说，别看卢卡那小子一幅愣头青的模样，他心里其实装得下很多东西。他跟你说过那只机械手的来历吗，{NICKNAME}？
t_en: ...Huh, he may be a tough guy on the surface, but he's got his secrets. Did he tell you about the origins of his mechanical arm?

t_ch: 和史瓦罗对决之前提了两句。
t_en: He mentioned something before he fought Svarog.

t_ch: 噢，那他应该没给你们详细讲述当时的情况。
t_en: Ah, then he probably didn't describe the scene in detail.

t_ch: 没印象了。
t_en: I don't remember.

t_ch: 噢，那不如让我来给你讲讲吧。
t_en: Well, let me tell you about it.

t_ch: 那大概是…六年，不对，七年之前？当时我带着一帮弟兄在隔壁熔锤镇巡逻，正好路过一个不起眼的工匠铺子。
t_en: That was... six? Seven years ago? I was patrolling with some comrades in the neighboring town of Moltammerville — we passed an ordinary mechanics shop.

t_ch: 看店的是个一脸严肃的男人，估摸比我小几岁，但头发胡子全白了。他身边坐着个红毛小子，在一旁有气无力地干着杂活，眼睛里一点儿神都没有。
t_en: There was a serious-looking fella looking after the shop. He was probably a few years younger than me, but his hair and beard were white as snow. There was a young lad doing odd jobs — he looked pretty exhausted.

t_ch: …不重要的细节我就省去了。总之，后来我说服了店主，让那个红毛小子停下他不喜欢干的工匠活，跟我学些技能。
t_en: Anyway, I convinced the shopkeeper to let him come with me.

t_ch: 卢卡这小子，学得很快。一开始，我也搞不清楚他喜不喜欢我教他的东西——搏击，兵器，战略之类的——他也不怎么吭声，只是闷着头学。
t_en: Luka was a fast learner. At first, I couldn't figure out if he liked what I was teaching him — fighting, weapons, strategy, etc. He didn't talk a lot — just kept his head down.

t_ch: 后来，大概是我收他为徒一年多以后吧…在一次熔锤镇的保卫战里，卢卡为了从裂界手里救下几个镇民，被弄废了一只手臂。
t_en: Then... about a year after I took him in, Luka lost one of his arms while rescuing townsfolk from the Fragmentum in Moltammerville.

t_ch: …那次他差点就没挺过来，连着昏厥了两三天。我在他病床前守着，心里火急火燎的，又内疚又着急。结果，你猜…他醒来以后第一句话是什么？
t_en: ...He almost didn't make it — he was unconscious for two or three days. I sat next to his bed, anxious and guilty. Guess what he said to me after he woke up?

t_ch: 他说了什么？
t_en: ...Go on.

t_ch: 「师傅，我很高兴！」
t_en: "Master, I feel great!"

t_ch: ——他说那话的时候全身缠满绷带，血肉模糊的…但那是他第一次冲我露出笑脸，那表情我现在闭上眼还能瞧见。
t_en: He had bandages all over his body — most of his wounds hadn't healed yet... But, it was the first time he'd ever smiled at me. I still remember that expression.

t_ch: 这个故事想表达的是？
t_en: And the moral of the story is...?

t_ch: 噢，抱歉，我每次讲这故事都有点儿忘乎所以，哈哈哈。
t_en: Oh, sorry, I get a little carried away when I tell this story, hahaha.

t_ch: 原来他是这样的人…
t_en: Guess that kind of sums him up, huh...

t_ch: 看不出来，是吧？
t_en: Can't tell from looking at him, right?

t_ch: 我想说的就是，卢卡那小子把身边人的命看得比自己重要，从小就是这样。
t_en: What I'm trying to say is: Luka views other people's lives as more important than his own. That's how he's always been.

t_ch: 所以从他嘴里说出「都是为了自我修炼」这种话…多半是糊弄人的。他心里肯定瞒着什么事没跟你们说。
t_en: So when he says stuff like "this is for training" — that's probably a lie. He's hiding something from you alright.

t_ch: 年轻人的事，我也不想多掺和了。我有信心，你跟希儿绝对有办法搞清楚。
t_en: Anyway, I'm too old to be getting involved in this stuff. I'm sure you and Seele can sort this by yourselves.

t_ch: 不过，你们得答应我件事，别让那小子太下不来台，行吗？毕竟他是磐岩镇的搏击冠军，孩子们的偶像——这光辉形象还得想办法维持下去啊，哈哈哈。
t_en: One thing I will ask — please try not to embarrass him too much, okay? He's the fighting champion of Boulder Town, after all — an idol for all the children. He needs to keep up that image, haha.

t_ch: 欸，你来了！快，他走得不急，现在出发应该还能赶上。
t_en: Oh, you're here! Come on — he wasn't in a hurry so we should be able to catch up easy enough.

t_ch: 走吧。
t_en: Let's go.

t_ch: 嗯。
t_en: Mm.

t_ch: 稍等…
t_en: Hold on...

t_ch: …哎呀，可别墨迹太久，时间不等人！
t_en: ...That is, unless you're planning a big delay!

t_ch: 怎么了？快去俱乐部吧，不然比赛就该结束了。
t_en: Huh? Hurry and run to the Club now. Otherwise, the fight'll be over when you get there.

t_ch: 卢卡那小子的对手一般都撑不了几个回合，哈哈哈哈。
t_en: Luka's opponents usually don't last long, hahaha. Go!

t_ch: 我事先就有种不好的预感…卢卡这家伙，果然想一出是一出。
t_en: I have a bad feeling about this... Luka always does things on the spur of the moment.

t_ch: 感觉他有些亢奋…
t_en: He does seem very excited...

t_ch: …可能是因为刚从擂台上下来，兴奋劲还没过？不过卢卡做事一向风风火火，我早就习惯了。
t_en: ...Maybe he's on a victory high? Or maybe not. Either way, it's classic Luka.

t_ch: 感觉他很热情。
t_en: He does seem very passionate...

t_ch: …是热情过度了吧？不过卢卡做事一向风风火火，我早就习惯了。
t_en: ...Too passionate. Either way, it's classic Luka — I'm used to it by now.

t_ch: 别看他现在这样，要是真上了前线，我愿意把性命托付给他。光是「地火」一个组织里，欠他一条命的人就不止几十个…更别提下层区的平民了。
t_en: Still, when it comes to fighting on the front lines, you can put your life in his hands. There's a dozen of us in Wildfire who are still in his debt... not to mention ordinary Underworlders.

t_ch: …话是这么说，真不知道这次他又想出了什么鬼点子。只能走一步看一步了。
t_en: ...That's a story for another day. I have no idea what he's up to this time, we'll just have to wait and see.

t_ch: 卢卡那家伙，怎么总是悄咪咪地跑回镇上？
t_en: Why does Luka always come sneaking back into town like this?

t_ch: 我很久没见过那家伙了…希望他这段时间有所成长。
t_en: I haven't seen him for a while... I hope he's grown up a bit since then.

t_ch: 史瓦罗的地盘，咱们到了。接下来去哪？
t_en: This is Svarog's turf. Where to now?

t_ch: 别着急，跟着我就行了。先去找一个叫卡西迪的机械师吧，我之前已经跟他打过招呼了。走，这边！
t_en: Don't worry, just follow me. We need to find a mechanic called Cassidy first — I've hit him up before. This way!

t_ch: 卢卡！可算来了，我等你半天了……
t_en: Luka! You're finally here. I've been waiting for you for ages...

t_ch: 放松点，哥们！我答应你的事一定说到做到，犯不着紧张成这样。
t_en: Relax, bro! I'm a man of my word. No need to stress out.

t_ch: 这两位是…哎哟，这不是希儿小姐吗？！我这点鸡毛蒜皮的小事居然惊动了「地火」的两位干部……
t_en: And these two are... Oh, Miss Seele!? To think that two senior Wildfire members are concerned with my trivial problems...

t_ch: 噢，{F#她们}{M#他们}俩是顺路来帮忙的，别介意！你把遇上的事给我们讲明白了就行。
t_en: They just came along to help. Don't mind them! Just tell us exactly what happened.

t_ch: 顺路帮忙……
t_en: Came along to help, huh...

t_ch: 那、那我就说了，你们别笑话我…我是个业余机械师，平时没事就喜欢收集些废件，自己搞些拼装机器人的小活，也算是乐在其中了。
t_en: O—Okay... this may sound strange but — I'm an amateur mechanic. I like collecting scrap in my free time, doing odd jobs like putting robots together... that sort of thing.

t_ch: 可就在前几天，我最满意的两个机器人伙伴突然不见了！我平时出去都会把它们留在帐篷里，这聚落周围也都是熟人，以前从来没出过类似的事……
t_en: But a few days ago, two of my robots suddenly disappeared! They were my pride and joy. I usually leave them in the tent when I head out — everyone in the settlement knows each other. This has never happened before...

t_ch: 我怀疑是有人盯上了我的机器伙伴，趁我不在偷偷把它们带走了——虽然我不知道那家伙是怎么做到的，难道是篡改了系统？不可能啊……
t_en: Someone must have set their sights on my robots and stolen them while I was gone — I just don't know how they managed it. Did they change the programming? But that's not possible...

t_ch: 也许它们只是搭伙去散步了。
t_en: Maybe they just went out for a walk together.

t_ch: 怎、怎么可能？！您别开玩笑了，看不出来我是真的很痛心吗……
t_en: H—Hey, stop kidding around! Can't you tell I have a serious problem on my hands...?

t_ch: 为什么不可能？
t_en: Why isn't that possible?

t_ch: 呃…没别的意思，我单纯觉得这聚落里的人没那个本事。
t_en: *sigh* It's not impossible, I just don't think there's anyone in the settlement capable of something like that.

t_ch: 原来如此！丢了两台机器人，是吧？那你手上有什么线索没？或者只是合理的猜想也行。我们想帮忙调查，总得找个入手点吧？
t_en: I see, two robots... Do you have any clues? Any reasonable guesses? We need to have an opening to start the investigation.

t_ch: 呃…非要说的话，有一件事我到现在都想不太明白。
t_en: Not really... but here's the most mysterious part of all this:

t_ch: 我那两部失窃的机器里有一台无人机，我给它起名叫「小飞」。我给「小飞」装了报警器，没权限的人只要一靠近它就会嗡嗡叫个没完——那音量，保证整个聚落的人都能听见。
t_en: One of the machines they stole was a drone — I call it Little Fay. Little Fay has an alarm that buzzes non-stop as soon as any unauthorized people get close. It's guaranteed to wake up the entire settlement.

t_ch: 但是机器人失窃的时候，我压根没听见「小飞」的报警声…这事我到现在都想不明白。
t_en: But I didn't hear Little Fay's alarm at all... I still can't figure out how that happened.

t_ch: 故障了？
t_en: Maybe it stopped working?

t_ch: 不可能！我走之前「小飞」还好好的，肯定不可能突然出问题。
t_en: No way! Little Fay was shipshape — not a hitch nor glitch in sight.

t_ch: 也许聚落里真有机器人高手。
t_en: Maybe there's a true robotics master in the settlement.

t_ch: 不…不可能！{F#姐们}{M#哥们}，这你得信我，我对住在这附近的人可是再熟不过了。
t_en: T—Trust me! I know the people here — it's extremely unlikely.

t_ch: 嗯，原本应该发出警报的无人机突然成了哑炮…值得留意。还有别的补充吗？
t_en: Hmm, a drone that was supposed to sound an alarm suddenly went silent... Worth remembering. Anything else?

t_ch: 我想想…哦，还有一个细节，不知道能不能派上用场——
t_en: Let me think... Oh, there is one other thing, but I don't know if it's useful...

t_ch: 我给「小飞」装了个追踪仪，只要它离「大奔」——哦，「大奔」是另外那台机器人的名字——只要它俩之间距离过远，「小飞」就会开启自动寻路，飞到「大奔」身边。
t_en: Little Fay also has a tracker. If it gets too far from Big Ben — oh, Big Ben is the name of the other robot — if there's too much distance between them, Little Fay will automatically start routing and fly to Big Ben's location.

t_ch: …就这些了，我试过自己去找线索，但根本不知道该从哪下手。果然，专业的事还是得留给专业的人做……
t_en: ...That's all I suppose. I tried to find clues myself, but I don't know where to start. This job needs real professionals...

t_ch: 嗯，嗯…行，我明白了！放心吧哥们，我们保证帮你把机器人找回来。
t_en: Mm-hmm... Cool, we've got this! Don't worry, bro. We promise we'll find your robots!

t_ch: …喂，你哪来的自信？
t_en: ...Hey, how can you be so sure?

t_ch: 相信我，希儿！
t_en: Trust me, Seele!

t_ch: 别担心，卡西迪，你的事就交给「地火」吧。你就在这儿好好歇着，别被坏心情毁了一整天！
t_en: Don't worry, Cassidy — Wildfire's on the case. You just rest up here, and don't let this ruin your day!

t_ch: …喂，刚才那是啥情况？你就那么确定咱们能帮他找到东西？
t_en: What was that all about? You're <unbreak>100%</unbreak> certain we're gonna find those robots for him?

t_ch: 别着急，希儿！你先听听我的推论。
t_en: Slow down, Seele! Listen to my analysis first.

t_ch: 想趁人不注意偷走两台机器人可是个大工程，何况其中一台还装了报警器。所以，我猜咱们的小贼朋友压根没把「小飞」带走。
t_en: It's a big job to steal two robots without anyone noticing, especially when one of them has an alarm. I'm guessing that our thief friend never even took Little Fay.

t_ch: 你的意思是……
t_en: You mean...

t_ch: 那家伙肯定知道「小飞」装了报警系统，所以干脆决定先用暴力手段把它弄坏。
t_en: I think the thief knew that Little Fay had an alarm, so they decided to break it.

t_ch: 但他又怕卡西迪能把「小飞」修好，然后用追踪功能寻找「大奔」…所以他把损坏的无人机丢到了某个地方，自己带着「大奔」跑路了。
t_en: But they also knew that Cassidy could fix Little Fay, and use its tracker function to find Big Ben... The thief must have tossed the broken drone somewhere and only made off with Big Ben.

t_ch: …欸，你们怎么愣住了？倒是发表下看法啊。
t_en: ...Hey, why are you both looking at me like that? Say something.

t_ch: 不会是你干的吧…
t_en: Whoa, hold up! That sounds sus. You didn't do it, did you?

t_ch: 哈哈哈，看来你挺认同我的推断，是吧？
t_en: Hahaha, so you agree with my reasoning, right?

t_ch: 确实符合逻辑。
t_en: It does make sense...

t_ch: 当真？要是有什么漏洞，你们可得及时指出来啊。
t_en: Seriously? You've gotta tell me if there are holes in my logic.

t_ch: 希儿怎么看？
t_en: What do you think, Seele?

t_ch: 我感觉…卢卡的推论还挺合理的。前提是窃贼确实只有一个人。
t_en: I think... Luka's analysis makes sense, provided there was only one thief.

t_ch: 没想到，原来你不止四肢发达啊。
t_en: So, you do have a brain somewhere in all that muscle.

t_ch: 哈哈哈，三肢发达才准确。既然你们都同意我的推断，那干脆就顺着这个思路找起来吧。
t_en: Hahaha, I'm not just biceps and steel, y'know. Well, if you all agree with what I said, let's go prove it.

t_ch: 这地方最不缺的就是被四处乱丢的无人机。动嘴不如动腿，走吧！
t_en: Missing drones are a bit of a regular occurrence down here. Come on, move those legs!

t_ch: 看，这儿就有一架无人机！不过该怎么判断是不是卡西迪丢的那架呢……
t_en: Look, there's a drone over there! But how can we tell if it's the one Cassidy lost...

t_ch: 这里…看，好像有人故意把外壳上的漆字给刮花了，而且还没刮干净。
t_en: Here, look... someone deliberately scratched off the paintwork — didn't even do a good job of it.

t_ch: 喔，确实！好眼力啊希儿。不管是谁干的，当时肯定着急得很。
t_en: Oh, you're right! You've got a good eye, Seele! Whoever did it must've been in a hurry.

t_ch: 得想办法把这小家伙修好——{NICKNAME}，能帮把手吗？
t_en: We need to fix this little guy up — {NICKNAME}, can you lend a hand?

t_ch: 成了！小家伙又能飞了。
t_en: Done! The little one can fly again!

t_ch: 哔——叭！「小飞」——已完成重启！
t_en: Beep - bing! Little Fay re-initialization complete!

t_ch: 哈，运气不赖，居然真让咱们找到了。
t_en: Haha, we really found it! Pretty lucky if you ask me.

t_ch: 检索「大奔」的系统信号中……
t_en: Scanning for signal from Big Ben...

t_ch: 你们仔细听！这肯定就是卡西迪之前说的追踪系统。
t_en: Listen! That must be the tracking system Cassidy mentioned.

t_ch: 检索失败。「大奔」与本机距离过远。需要开启追踪功能。
t_en: Signal not found. Big Ben is currently too far away from this unit. Tracking function must be enabled.

t_ch: 居然还真有追踪功能…看看它到底灵不灵光吧。
t_en: It really does have a tracking function... Let's see how useful it is.

t_ch: 启动「小飞」的追踪功能。
t_en: Activate Little Fay's tracking function.

t_ch: 喂，它要飞走了！快跟上去！
t_en: Hey, it's flying away! Follow it!

t_ch: 喂，朋友！
t_en: Hey, friend!

t_ch: …谁、谁在那边？！
t_en: Wh—Who's there!?

t_ch: 红色的系带…地、地火？！你们来这儿干嘛？我…呃，我过得挺好的，不用你们帮忙！你、你们赶紧走吧，去管别人的闲事！
t_en: A red ribbon... W—Wildfire!? Ha! How did you...? I'm doing just fine — no monsters around here. Why don't you go bother someone else?

t_ch: 哼，每天净想着不劳而获，没事就干些小偷小摸的勾当…过得能不好吗？
t_en: Hmph, shying away from honest work, indulging in petty crime — of course you're "doing just fine."

t_ch: 哈，这我就得反驳你一句了，希儿！你看他身边那台「大奔」，这水平怎么也不止小偷小摸了吧？
t_en: Uh, I'm not sure this falls under "petty crime," Seele — that's a whole robot he's got next to him. It's Big Ben alright.

t_ch: 等、等会儿，你们怎么知道这机器人的名字？难道你们是——
t_en: H—Hold on a second. Who told you the name of this robot? Don't tell me it's—

t_ch: ——你猜对了，就是卡西迪让我们来的！把你偷走的东西交出来，不然就等着见识冠军的拳头吧！
t_en: That's right. Cassidy sent us here! Hand over what you stole, or be prepared to face the fist of a champion!

t_ch: …去你们的吧！事已至此，我也豁出去了！反正要是这事传出去，我也别想在这附近混了…老子今天就跟你们拼了！
t_en: ...F—Fist of a champion, huh? Hmph, I've come this far — I won't be able to show my face around here if news gets out... Do your worst!

t_ch: 「大奔」，准备跟我上！
t_en: Big Ben, let's get him!

t_ch: 死不悔改啊，这家伙——那就不用再跟他废话了，卢卡！
t_en: Going all the way, huh? Enough talk then — Luka, let's get him!

t_ch: 哈，这下想到一块去了！来吧，今天必须让你尝点苦头！
t_en: Ha! I was thinking the exact same thing! Here, let me punch some sense into you!

t_ch: …我错了！大哥大姐们，我知道错了！你们放我一马吧！
t_en: ...Spare me! Please, all of you, I shouldn't have stolen anything — I admit! Give me a second chance!

t_ch: 哈哈哈，这就认输了？刚才的气势跑哪去了？
t_en: Hahaha, you're admitting defeat? What happened to all that bravado you had a few moments ago?

t_ch: <size=36>「熔锤出身的勇士——」</size>
t_en: <size=36>"The Moltammerville warrior..."</size>

t_ch: <size=36>「无敌的磐岩冠军——」</size>
t_en: <size=36>"...the peerless Boulder champion..."</size>

t_ch: <size=36>「——再次维护了公正跟和平！」</size>
t_en: <size=36>"...upholding justice and peace once again!"</size>

t_ch: 漂亮！咱们这位神偷朋友认怂了，「大奔」看上去也完好无损。冠军试炼的第一步圆满完成！
t_en: Nice! Our thieving friend surrendered, and Big Ben looks to be in one piece. The first step of the champion's training is complete!

t_ch: …欸？你们这是什么反应，不能表现得开心点嘛？
t_en: ...Huh, why are you two so straight-faced? Can't you look a little happy?

t_ch: （什么也不说。）
t_en: (Say nothing)

t_ch: …刚才那算怎么回事？「熔锤镇出身的勇士」，后面还有一大堆……
t_en: ...What was that supposed to be? "The Moltammerville warrior"...?

t_ch: 「熔锤镇出身的勇士…」
t_en: "The Moltammerville warrior..."

t_ch: …我刚想问，刚才那算怎么回事？
t_en: Yeah, I was gonna ask... what was all that supposed to be?

t_ch: 噢，你说那个？没什么，一句口号而已，别放在心上。走吧，咱们赶紧先把「大奔」还给卡西迪。
t_en: Oh, that? Nothing, just a slogan — don't take it too seriously. Let's get Big Ben back to Cassidy.

t_ch: 好吧，先把手头要紧事结了。但是丑话说在前头，我们可没那么健忘……
t_en: Fine, let's finish up here. But I'm gonna put it out there — we're not gonna forget those lines of yours anytime soon...
Beep... boop...

t_ch: 大奔想家了。大奔想和小飞聊天。大奔想和卡西迪一起玩。
t_en: Big Ben misses home. Big Ben wants to talk with Little Fay. Big Ben wants to play with Cassidy.

t_ch: 走吧，我们回家。
t_en: Come on, let's go home.

t_ch: 回家。回家。
t_en: Go home. Go home.

t_ch: 大奔——你回来了，大奔！
t_en: Big Ben! You're back!

t_ch: 哔——啵。卡西迪。小飞。卡西迪。
t_en: Beep... boop. Cassidy. Little Fay. Cassidy.

t_ch: 真不知道该怎么感谢你们，卢卡，希儿，还有…外来者{F#姐妹}{M#兄弟}！这下我的生活终于能回归正轨了……
t_en: I don't know how I can ever thank you... Luka, Seele, and... {F#miss}{M#mister} outsider! My life can finally get back on track now...

t_ch: 怎么样，我没说大话吧？「地火」的干部向来说到做到。
t_en: See? I kept my word, right? Wildfire's senior members always deliver what we promise.

t_ch: 我从来没怀疑过你，卢卡兄弟！欠你的这个人情我会一直记得…不介意的话，现在我想去给小飞和大奔做一次系统彻查。
t_en: I've never doubted you, Luka! I'll remember this forever... Right, time to take Little Fay and Big Ben for a systems check — until next time!

t_ch: 行啊，你快去吧！咱们回头见。
t_en: Yeah, go ahead! We'll catch up later.

t_ch: …神清气爽！帮上别人忙的感觉总是特别充实，你们觉得呢？
t_en: ...That felt great! It's such a wholesome feeling to help other people, don't you think?

t_ch: 老实说，这点事…我感觉你一个人也能搞定。
t_en: To be honest, I think you could've handled it yourself.

t_ch: 不过你之前说，这只是完成了第一步…我斗胆问问，你这「试炼」一共分了几步？
t_en: You said this was the first step, right...? So just how many steps does your training have, exactly?

t_ch: 三步，一共就三步。放心，保证不会占用你们太多时间！
t_en: Three. Just three steps. Trust me, it won't take too much of your time!

t_ch: 接下来去哪？
t_en: Where to next?

t_ch: 别着急，我正要说呢。
t_en: Easy, I'm about to tell you.

t_ch: 我渴望战斗！
t_en: I THIRST FOR BATTLE!!!

t_ch: 哈哈，虽然我刚认识你没多久，但是你这股精神头我很欣赏！
t_en: Hahaha, I like your spirit! Shame I didn't run into you sooner.

t_ch: 我更关心报酬…
t_en: I care more about what I can get as rewards...

t_ch: 呃，这事要不晚点再说……
t_en: Um, maybe we can talk about that later...

t_ch: 走吧，两位！下一步，咱们要把这聚落里的裂界渣滓都给清理干净！
t_en: Let's go! Next, we're gonna clear up all the Fragmentum cronies still left in the settlement!

t_ch: …哈？！裂界？但我记得这片聚落——
t_en: ...Huh? Fragmentum? But the settlement...

t_ch: ——没有受到裂界影响，对吧？你没记错，这里的裂口就是最近才被撕开的，昨天奥列格师傅才收到探子的消息。
t_en: ...Wasn't affected by the Fragmentum, right? You're not wrong. The fissure here only recently opened up. Master Oleg got the intel from our network just yesterday.

t_ch: 我特地和他说，希望能把这个问题交给我处理。你看，这第二步试炼的内容不就有了吗？
t_en: I asked him to leave this case to me. Perfect for the second step of the training, right?

t_ch: …真佩服你们师徒俩，心够大的。既然事关裂界，那的确一秒都拖不起。怪物出没的地方在哪，赶紧带路吧。
t_en: Like master, like student... I admire your attitude. In any case, if this concerns the Fragmentum, then there's no time to lose. Where are the monsters? Lead the way.

t_ch: 这就出发，跟我来！
t_en: Way ahead of you. Follow me!

t_ch: 这个数量…确定裂界怪物是最近才涌进来的吗？
t_en: There are so many of them... Are you sure the fissure only recently opened up?

t_ch: 消息应该无误。看来史瓦罗大佬也放松警惕了啊…居然放任这些家伙在他的地盘捣乱！
t_en: Our intel should have been accurate... Seems like Boss Svarog must have let his guard down — these guys are messing up his turf!

t_ch: 没什么好说的，我们先把这片区域清理干净吧。
t_en: Enough talk. Let's clean up this area.

t_ch: 唷，怪不得前面那些小喽啰那么活跃，原来身后藏着这么个大家伙啊！
t_en: Ah, no wonder these minions are so active — there's a big fella hiding behind them!

t_ch: 这家伙应该就是源头了吧？干掉它，这片区域的裂界活动应该就能消停一阵了——
t_en: So, this guy is the source of it all, right? If we take him out, the Fragmentum activity in this area should subside for a while...

t_ch: ——你的第二个试炼也就算完成了，是吧？
t_en: ...And trial two will be complete.

t_ch: 哈哈哈，没错！怎么，你这不是也热血沸腾了吗，希儿？
t_en: Hahaha, that's right! What's up? Starting to get into it, Seele?

t_ch: …太久没好好活动筋骨了，今天正好拿这家伙热热身！
t_en: ...It's been too long since I had a good workout. This will be a nice warm-up!

t_ch: 好！{NICKNAME}——等你准备好了我们就上！
t_en: Alright! {NICKNAME} — we'll charge in once you're ready!

t_ch: 这家伙还真难缠…幸好咱们不用单打独斗。
t_en: Phew! That was a tough one... Good teamwork, guys.

t_ch: 这样就行了吧，卢——
t_en: Luka, is that everyth—

t_ch: <size=36>「熔锤出身的勇士——」</size>
t_en: <size=36>"The Moltammerville warrior..."</size>

t_ch: <size=36>「无敌的磐岩冠军——」</size>
t_en: <size=36>"...the peerless Boulder champion..."</size>

t_ch: <size=36>「——让世界上的邪恶无处遁形！」</size>
t_en: <size=36>"Defeater of evil throughout the world!"</size>
You...

t_ch: 你没事吧？！
t_en: Are you okay!?

t_ch: 这是什么行为艺术吗？
t_en: ...Is this some kind of performance art?

t_ch: …一惊一乍的，你不是得了什么毛病吧？
t_en: ...Why are you so hyped? What's going on?

t_ch: 「——让世界上的邪恶无处遁形！」
t_en: "Defeater of evil throughout the world!"

t_ch: …你就别学他了！
t_en: ...Don't copy him!

t_ch: 怎么搞的，你今天怎么老是一惊一乍的？不是得了什么毛病吧？
t_en: Seriously, why are you so hyped up today?

t_ch: 哈哈哈，就是一套正常的庆祝仪式，也不用那么大反应吧？
t_en: Hahaha, it's just a normal victory celebration. No need to be so alarmed.

t_ch: …你管这叫「正常」？要是在擂台上也就算了…不对，哪怕是赢了比赛以后，我也从来没见你喊过那么尴尬的台词……
t_en: ...You call that normal? I might have let it slide if it was in the cage... On second thought, no, that was cringe even by Fight Club standards.

t_ch: 别太纠结了，希儿！我跟你保证，我肩上这颗脑袋清醒得很。你看，咱们这不又帮下层区除掉了个大祸害吗？
t_en: Don't get so worked up, Seele! I'm thinking clearly! Plus, didn't we just get rid of another major threat to the Underworld?

t_ch: 话是不假。但一码归一码，你的这个新「习惯」，我实在是……
t_en: True, but quit changing the subject! This new "routine" of yours is just...

t_ch: 哈哈哈，放心！最多也就持续到这次试炼结束，我保证——哎唷，我今天是不是「保证」太多次了？这可不是啥好习惯……
t_en: Hahaha, don't worry! I promise you, it'll only last till the end of my training. Actually, have I been making too many promises today? That's not a good habit...

t_ch: 反正我安排的试炼就只剩最后一步了。两位，辛苦你们陪我再走一段！
t_en: Anyway, there's only one more step in my training plan. Come on guys, help me wrap it up!

t_ch: …都走到这儿了，现在半途而废我自己也会觉得可惜。说吧，你还想去干啥？
t_en: *sigh* ...I guess we're in too deep, better let it run its course. So tell us, what else is on the schedule.

t_ch: 这场修炼的最后一步——就是挑战史瓦罗大佬！
t_en: The final step in this training — is to challenge boss Svarog!

t_ch: 你还敢说自己没毛病？！
t_en: Oh boy...

t_ch: 加油，我支持你！
t_en: Noice! Count me in!

t_ch: 你还敢说自己没毛病？！
t_en: Are you sure you're thinking clearly!?

t_ch: 冷静，别上头，希儿！都说了我很清醒，我说的每个字都是认真的！
t_en: Whoa, whoa, stay cool, Seele. Like I said, I'm thinking clearly. Every word I said is serious!

t_ch: 这次试炼的最终目标，我几天前就已经定下了。你想啊，下层区还有比史瓦罗大佬更值得挑战的对手吗？
t_en: I set this as the final goal of my training a few days ago. Think about it — is there any Underworld opponent worth challenging more than Boss Svarog?

t_ch: …不行，我不能答应，你这就是自找不痛快！且不说你有没有胜算，「地火」和史瓦罗的关系才刚修复……
t_en: ...No, I can't agree to this, you're asking for trouble! Forget about winning or losing — the relationship between Wildfire and Svarog only just got patched up...

t_ch: 我又不是要去挑衅他，你别把事想复杂了。我是要请他跟我来一场公平公正的对决，就像在拳台上那样！
t_en: I'm not going to rile him up! Don't make this more complicated than it is. I'm just inviting him to have a fair fight with me, like how you'd have in the cage!

t_ch: 你疯了？史瓦罗是个机器人，他才不懂什么搏击台上的礼仪！跟咱们不一样，他的拳头可不是肉长的——
t_en: Are you mad? Svarog is a robot! He doesn't understand the rules of the cage! He's different from us. His fists are made of steel—

t_ch: …那我们可算是棋逢对手，对吧？
t_en: ...That means we're evenly matched, right?

t_ch: 你知道我是什么意思，别抖机灵！我只是觉得，这事完全不符合逻辑……
t_en: You know what I mean — don't act smart with me! Ugh, you don't make any sense...

t_ch: 哈哈哈，我理解你的担心，希儿！但是你得知道，我是个格斗家…不断修炼、不断变强可是我的职责。
t_en: Hahaha, I understand what you're saying, Seele! But you know, I'm a fighter... It's my duty to keep training and get stronger.

t_ch: 脑子里一直有个想挑战的强者，但自己却一直没法接近他…那感觉可太憋屈了！多亏你们跟史瓦罗达成了和解，我才能有这个机会——我绝对不想错过！
t_en: It's a terrible feeling to have a powerful opponent in your head but never be able to get close to him. It's all thanks to your patching things up with Svarog that I'm able to have this opportunity now — I'm not gonna miss it for the world!

t_ch: 答应他吧，希儿。
t_en: Let him do it, Seele.

t_ch: 史瓦罗应该会顾及我们的面子…
t_en: Svarog might go easy because we're here...

t_ch: …我知道了，好吧。那我们去见史瓦罗，跟他聊聊。
t_en: ...Alright. Fine. We'll go find Svarog and have a nice chat with him.

t_ch: 丑话说前面，要是他懒得搭理你这个「挑战者」…我们立马就离开这儿。
t_en: Lemme say this first — if he doesn't wanna deal with you as a challenger, then we're getting out of there ASAP.

t_ch: 哈，放心，我会说服他的！不过你说得对，要是他不在兴头上，那也不好强求。
t_en: Haha, don't worry, I'll convince him! But you're right. If he's not in the mood for it, then I won't force him.

t_ch: 我没怎么跟史瓦罗大佬接触过，这次还辛苦你们带路了。出发吧！
t_en: I haven't really worked with boss Svarog before, so please, lead the way. Let's go!

t_ch: 开拓者。「地火」的希儿。还有……
t_en: The Trailblazer, Seele of Wildfire, and...

t_ch: 唷，幸会，史瓦罗大佬！我叫卢卡，和希儿一样是「地火」的干部。
t_en: Hey! An honor to meet you, Boss Svarog! I'm Luka, a senior member of Wildfire, just like Seele.

t_ch: 调用后台数据库。检索中……
t_en: Accessing back-end database. Querying...

t_ch: 检索完成。卢卡，熔锤镇出身，磐岩镇搏击擂台赛二十八届冠军得主，「地火」代理首领奥列格的爱徒。
t_en: Query complete. Luka, born in Moltammerville, winner of the <unbreak>28</unbreak>th Boulder Town Fight King Challenge. Beloved student of Oleg, acting chief of Wildfire.

t_ch: …嚯，厉害！谢谢你替我省去了一大段自我介绍。
t_en: ...Wow, impressive! Thank you for saving me the self-introduction.

t_ch: 「地火」的卢卡，你来找我有什么目的？
t_en: Luka of Wildfire, what is the purpose of your visit?

t_ch: 直奔主题，正合我意——我是来挑战你的，史瓦罗大佬！
t_en: Straight to the point, just the way I like it — I'm here to challenge you, Boss Svarog!

t_ch: …挑战？
t_en: ...Challenge?

t_ch: 意图不明。{NICKNAME}、「地火」的希儿——你们能否做出解释？
t_en: Intent unclear. {NICKNAME}, Seele of Wildfire — can you provide an explanation?

t_ch: 我很难解释…
t_en: Not really...

t_ch: …也别看我，都是他自己一拍脑门想出的主意。同不同意都看你，史瓦罗。
t_en: ...Don't look at me. He's the one who came up with this idea. It's up to you to accept it or not, Svarog.

t_ch: 他*这里*有点问题。
t_en: I think he might have hit his head.

t_ch: …都是他自己一拍脑门想出的主意。同不同意都看你，史瓦罗。
t_en: ...He's the one who came up with this idea. It's up to you to accept it or not, Svarog.

t_ch: 答应他吧！
t_en: Just accept!

t_ch: 喂，你怎么还煽上风了！
t_en: Hey, why are you fanning the flames!?

t_ch: …咳，这都是他自己一拍脑门想出的主意。同不同意都看你，史瓦罗。
t_en: *sigh* He's the one who came up with this idea. It's up to you to accept it or not, Svarog.

t_ch: 对！我也没什么想法，就是想跟这下层区公认的最强过上几招，看看自己到底有几斤几两。怎么样，有兴趣吗？
t_en: Yep! It's not that complicated. I just wanna go a few rounds with the one everyone agrees is the most powerful in the Underworld, and see where I stand. What do you say? Are you interested?

t_ch: 嗯…正在评估……
t_en: Hmm... evaluating...

t_ch: 对象动机，不明。敌意等级，零。测谎结果，通过。
t_en: Intention: Unclear. Enmity level: Zero. Lie-detection: Passed.

t_ch: 评估结果：对象确实只为「切磋」而来。
t_en: Evaluation completed. The opposing party only came to spar.

t_ch: ——我同意和你交手，「地火」的卢卡。
t_en: ...I agree to fight you, Luka of Wildfire.

t_ch: …哈？！等会儿，你这就答应他了？
t_en: ...Huh!? Hold on — you agree?

t_ch: 兵器结构与炮筒若长期不予使用，锈坏风险将大大提升。定期使用将减小风险概率，延长部件使用寿命。
t_en: If weapons and gun barrels are unused for prolonged periods of time, the risk of rust damages increases. Periodic use will reduce such risk and extend the life of these components.

t_ch: …啥？还能找个比这更烂的理由吗？
t_en: ...What? That's the worst excuse I've ever heard!

t_ch: 哈哈哈，看吧，都说了我绝对能说服他！
t_en: Hahaha, see? I told you I could convince him!

t_ch: ——那就拜托你当裁判了，希儿！俱乐部的规矩你再清楚不过了。
t_en: You can be our referee, Seele! You know the rules of the club inside out.

t_ch: …行吧，我知道了。既然你这么坚持……
t_en: ...Fine, I get it. If you insist...

t_ch: 双边选手——请到擂台两侧就位！
t_en: Competitors, take up positions!

t_ch: 与史瓦罗切磋
t_en: Spar with Svarog

t_ch: 我准备好了，史瓦罗！我们随时可以开始！
t_en: I'm ready, Svarog! We can start any time!

t_ch: 「地火」的卢卡——我问你，你那只机械义肢是何来头？
t_en: I too have a question, Luka of Wildfire — how did you acquire your mechanical arm?

t_ch: 哈，没想到这下层区还有你不知道的事，真是荣幸！
t_en: Ha! I'm honored to be one of the things in the Underworld that even you don't know about!

t_ch: 这条手臂——呵，其实没什么故事！在一场保卫战里走神的结果，连具体年月我都记不清了。
t_en: This arm — well, it's not that exciting. It's all because I got distracted in a defense campaign. I can't even remember the date of that fight.

t_ch: 重要的是，只有在丢了这只手之后…我才终于找到了意义，找到了活着的感觉！
t_en: More importantly... It was only after losing my arm that I found the meaning of life — what it really feels like to be alive!

t_ch: 这也是我现在站在这儿的原因——为了让自己觉得鲜活…也为了所有对我寄予厚望的家伙！
t_en: That's why I'm standing here right now — to feel alive, and also for everyone who put their hope and faith in me!

t_ch: 精彩的回答。我会认真对待这场「切磋」，「地火」的卢卡。
t_en: Excellent answer. I will treat this challenge seriously, Luka of Wildfire.

t_ch: 蓝方选手，机械聚落的史瓦罗——你准备好了吗？
t_en: In the blue corner, Svarog from the Robot Settlement — are you ready?

t_ch: 准备好了。
t_en: Ready.
